headline: Bill Granger obituary
name: William Granger
one_line: Australian chef who created the modern café breakfast template and pioneered avocado toast.

best_quote.speaker: Bill Granger
best_quote.text: Food should be about joy, it's the flowers of life.

headline: Wolfgang Schäuble obituary
name: Wolfgang Schäuble
one_line: German parliamentarian who navigated reunification and enforced austerity across Europe.

best_quote.speaker: Wolfgang Schäuble
best_quote.text: A cripple as chancellor? You have every right to ask that question.

notable_dates.event: Fall of the Berlin Wall, which triggered reunification negotiations Schäuble led

headline: Carlos Lyra obituary
name: Carlos Lyra
field: bossa nova singer, composer and guitarist
one_line: Bossa nova pioneer who fused samba, jazz and classical with political edge.

best_quote.text: Bossa nova is the discreet charm of the bourgeoisie … something that came from the middle class for the middle class. And I was determined to change that.
best_quote.speaker: Carlos Lyra

headline: Bob Quick obituary
name: Bob Quick
one_line: Senior trade union official and defender of NHS workers and healthcare.

best_quote.speaker: Bob Quick
best_quote.text: If I can't live near to the Med or by the sea, I shall have views of hills and castles.

notable_dates.event: Birth year 1955 (calculated from death aged 68 in 2023)

headline: Jacques Delors obituary
name: Jacques Lucien Jean Delors
one_line: Architect of the single European market and three-term Commission president.

best_quote.speaker: Jacques Delors
best_quote.text: National sovereignty no longer means much. In order to face American and Japanese challenges we need to be supranational.

notable_dates.event: Announced plans for European single market upon arrival at Commission, January 1985
Speech to TUC Congress on pro-labour legislation, 1988
Thatcher's 'No! No! No!' response to Delors in Commons, 1990

headline: Tony Oxley obituary
name: Tony Oxley
one_line: British jazz drummer who rejected convention for experimental improvisation and expanded percussion.

best_quote.speaker: Tony Oxley
best_quote.text: It doesn't take you long to realise that there's actually only one Art Blakey. If you can't find something a bit closer to yourself, then it's going to be a strained kind of life.

headline: Chris Parr obituary
name: Chris Parr
one_line: Theatre and television producer who championed new writers including Brenton and Edgar.
themes: agitprop theatre, 1960s British drama, television drama production, new writing]

best_quote.speaker: Kenneth Branagh
best_quote.text: humour and warmth and passion in working-class family life

headline: Jonathan Tubb obituary
name: Jonathan Nicholas Tubb
one_line: British Museum curator who championed archaeology over biblical texts in studying the ancient Levant

best_quote.speaker: Jonathan Tubb
best_quote.text: I like music that makes me think.

headline: Gao Yaojie obituary
name: Gao Yaojie
one_line: Fearless crusader exposing Aids spread through contaminated blood in rural China.

best_quote.text: When I entered, I saw that the mother had hanged herself and the body was stiff.
best_quote.speaker: Gao Yaojie

headline: Elliott Erwitt obituary
name: Elliott Erwitt
one_line: Master of candid photography who captured political figures and dogs with wry humour.

best_quote.speaker: Elliott Erwitt
best_quote.text: The whole point of taking pictures is so that you don't have to explain things with words.

notable_dates.event: Photograph of Che Guevara laughing and waving cigar in Havana, 1964

headline: Sir Tim Brighouse obituary
name: Timothy Robert Peter Brighouse
one_line: Education chief who transformed schools through respect, ideas and pragmatic improvement.

best_quote.speaker: Tim Brighouse
best_quote.text: It's about being human. Making a climate in which teachers and support staff feel honoured, valued and respected.

notable_dates.event: Attacked by Education Secretary John Patten at 1993 Tory conference

headline: Bob Johnson obituary
name: Bob Johnson
one_line: Steeleye Span guitarist who fused rock, blues and folk to revitalize traditional ballads.

best_quote.speaker: Peter Knight
best_quote.text: Steeleye Span was the perfect vehicle for his interest in traditional music and rock, and once he joined, he didn't want to do anything else.

headline: Norman Lear obituary
name: Norman Milton Lear
one_line: Creator of socially conscious sitcoms that transformed American television in the 1970s

best_quote.speaker: Norman Lear
best_quote.text: We had a Judeo-Christian ethic hanging around a couple of thousand years that didn't help erase racism. So the notion of a little half-hour comedy changing things is something I think is silly.

notable_dates.event: All in the Family pilot bought by CBS

headline: Jim Bennett obituary
name: James Arthur Bennett
field: museum curator and historian of science
one_line: Director of history of science museums who revolutionised understanding of science as hands-on practice.

best_quote.text: The calculators were worthless in a sense, because no one wants them – but that was precisely why it was essential to keep a collection of them.
best_quote.speaker: Jim Bennett

headline: Janet Alty obituary
name: Janet Alty
one_line: Green party councillor who transformed Leamington Spa local politics

best_quote.text: A woman who lived by the principles she promoted, she enthused many others to participate in local and national campaigns.
best_quote.speaker: obit writer

notable_dates.event: Stood down as Green party town councillor in Leamington Spa after eight years

headline: Otar Iosseliani obituary
name: Otar Iosseliani
one_line: Georgian director of abstract comedies exploring human absurdity and bourgeois emptiness

best_quote.speaker: Otar Iosseliani
best_quote.text: All my films are Georgian films. Although most of my films are set in provincial France, there is always a Georgian village behind the facades.

notable_dates.event: April (1961) filmed, later banned as formalism

headline: Lord Selkirk of Douglas obituary
name: James Alexander Douglas-Hamilton, Lord Selkirk of Douglas
one_line: Edinburgh MP and Scottish Office minister who transcended political divides across five decades.

best_quote.speaker: obit writer
best_quote.text: He had the old world manners of an aristocrat...he reportedly warned his assailant: 'I'm afraid I'm going to have to knock you out', before doing so.

notable_dates.event: Coronation of Queen Elizabeth II; page to the Queen Mother
Election as MP for Edinburgh West in October 1974
Rudolf Hess crash landing in Scotland, related to Douglas-Hamilton family history

headline: Andre Braugher obituary
name: Andre Keith Braugher
one_line: Detective Frank Pembleton in Homicide and Captain Ray Holt in Brooklyn Nine-Nine.
themes: American television drama, police procedurals, Shakespearean stage training, representation in broadcast media

best_quote.speaker: David Simon
best_quote.text: I've worked with a lot of wonderful actors. I'll never work with one better.

headline: Olive Barrett obituary
name: Olive Barrett
one_line: Jamaican migrant who built a life in post-war Coventry as a GEC factory worker.

headline: Michael Blakemore obituary
name: Michael Howell Blakemore
one_line: Director of Frayn's Copenhagen and Coward revivals, master of farce and contemporary plays.

best_quote.speaker: Michael Blakemore
best_quote.text: All I can say is, thank you America. And, when I say America, of course I mean New York. And when I say New York, I mean Broadway.

headline: Peter Betts obituary
name: Peter Betts
one_line: Chief EU negotiator who helped draft the Paris climate agreement.

best_quote.text: An old-fashioned civil servant, schooled in the need to speak truth to power, but to do so courteously, with impartial advice.
best_quote.speaker: obit writer
